[[File:Melzar the Mad chathead.png|left]]
'''Melzar the Mad''' is one of the three wizards, along with [[Thalzar]] and [[Lozar]], who escaped from [[Crandor]] when the [[Dragon (race)|dragon]] [[Elvarg]] destroyed it, and is the only one of them that is still alive. He has since lost his sanity, leading to his nickname, and has secluded himself and his [[Map part (Melzar)|piece of the map to Crandor]] in [[Melzar's Maze|his maze]] north-west of [[Rimmington]]. He is fought during the [[Dragon Slayer I]] quest.

In combat, Melzar can kick, punch, cast [[Fire Strike]] or a [[Curse spells|Curse spell]], or cast a [[spell]] that produces a [[cabbage]] in a random spot in the room. (Note that the cabbages '''CAN''' be used for [[food]].)

He built a [[Melzar's Maze|castle]] on top of the Crandorian refugee camp which Elvarg had destroyed then locked himself inside, sealing himself on the lowest floor. He then populated the castle with the undead forms of the Crandorians, having turned to [[necromancy]].{{CiteNPC|npc=Guildmaster|quote=Melzar built a castle on the site of the Crandorian refugee camp, north of Rimmington. He's locked himself in there and no one's seen him for years. The inside of his castle is like a maze, and is populated by undead monsters.|loc=Dragon Slayer I}} He also managed to trap a [[Lesser demon (Melzar's Maze)|lesser demon]], channelling its magic to aid his necromancy.{{CiteNPC|npc=Melzar the Mad|quote=...My ritual was a success! I have managed to subdue a lesser demon. I can channel it's magic to master the forces of life and death - as long as its chains hold...|loc=Melzar's notes}} He experimented with raising [[Zombie rat|rat corpses]], but found that the rats he experimented on did not obtain any of their living behaviours beyond basic movement.{{CiteNPC|npc=Melzar the Mad|quote=...On my fortieth attempt, the process was partially successful. The rat's flesh continues to decay, but it regains the faculty for movement it had in life. It is too early to say how many of its mental faculties have survived...[...]...Close study of the undead rat's behaviour reveal that no behavioural patterns acquired in life survive the process, even if the brain is perfectly preserved. I will have to find a way to conjure the deceased spirit separately...|loc=Melzar's notes}} He attempted to summon their [[Ghost (Melzar's Maze)|spiritual forms]], using personal items to bind the spirits.{{CiteNPC|npc=Melzar the Mad|quote=...I have searched the ruins of the refugee camp and found many personal items. I can use these to conjure up spirits of my deceased countrymen...|loc=Melzar's notes}} During his experiments, he started becoming obsessed with cabbages, and dreamed of the [[Stone of Jas|Cabbage of Jas]].{{CiteNPC|npc=Melzar the Mad|quote=...Had dream last night of great cabbage. Cabbage of Jas. Used by Gods to create all life and magic. all runes have part of its power. so with runes I can control power above magic...|loc=Melzar's notes}} He eventually succeeded in reanimating several [[Skeleton (Melzar's Maze)|skeletons]], but had trouble binding flesh to them. He selected two skeletons to grow flesh onto to turn them into [[zombie]]s.{{CiteNPC|npc=Melzar the Mad|quote=...I have succeeded in animating several of the skeletons, but without complete anatomies they cannot hold the spirits of my dead countrymen. Most will serve as guards, but I have selected two on which I will grow flesh...|loc=Melzar's notes}} He was unable to progress any further in his attempts at necromancy as he went mad not long after this.

According to [[Malignius Mortifer]], Melzar's madness was caused by a backfiring spell, which also affected his ability to get his spells to work as intended.{{CiteNPC|npc=Malignius Mortifer|quote=The forces required to wield power over the dead are very difficult to control. Melzar's concentration slipped, and his spell rebounded upon him. Although he did not die, his mind broke. Now he dwells amidst the beasts that fill his strange home, and when he attempts to do magic he gets... amusing results.}} However, his own [[Melzar's notes|notes]] indicate a descent into madness caused by grief and anger.
